English.amz.about.title: About Amazon
English.amz.area1.body: It is the core of Amazon's business, which includes its global online store and marketplace.
English.amz.area1.title: E-Commerce
English.amz.area2.body: It is Amazon's most profitable division and world leader in cloud computing with its cloud servers.
English.amz.area2.title: Cloud Computing
English.amz.area3.body: Competes with Netflix, Spotify and YouTube with Prime Video, Amazon Music, etc.
English.amz.area3.title: Streaming
English.amz.area4.body: Drives the smart home and automation with Alexa, Echo, etc.
English.amz.area4.title: AI
English.amz.hist.p1: Jeff Bezos founds Amazon in Seattle as an online bookstore, marking the beginning of one of the world's largest and most influential companies.
English.amz.hist.p2: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is launched, revolutionizing cloud computing and becoming Amazon's main profit engine.
English.amz.hist.p3: Amazon acquires Whole Foods Market, marking its strong entry into physical commerce and transforming the integration between physical stores and digital commerce.
English.amz.hist.p4: Amazon consolidates as a world leader in e-commerce, cloud computing, artificial intelligence and logistics.
English.amz.hist.title: History of Amazon
English.amz.mission.body: "To be the most customer-centric company in the world, where people can find and discover anything they want to buy online, offering the lowest possible prices."
English.amz.prod1.body: Amazon's subscription service with fast shipping, streaming and exclusive offers.
English.amz.prod1.title: Amazon Prime
English.amz.prod2.body: The world's most complete and adopted cloud platform.
English.amz.prod2.title: AWS
English.amz.tech1.body: Technological base for millions of companies and platforms.
English.amz.tech1.title: AWS
English.amz.tech2.body: Improves user experience and optimizes operations with Alexa and Computer Vision.
English.amz.tech2.title: AI
English.amz.tech3.body: Allows analyzing millions of data in real time with Amazon Redshift and EMR.
English.amz.tech3.title: Big Data
English.amz.vision.body: "To be the most customer-focused company on the planet, build a place where people can find and discover anything they want to buy online."
English.amz.what.body: Amazon is a multinational American company of e-commerce, technology and cloud services, founded in 1994 by Jeff Bezos. It is one of the world's largest and most influential companies.
English.acs.about.title: About ACS
English.acs.what.body: ACS (Actividades de Construcción y Servicios) is a Spanish group world leader in construction, infrastructure concessions and industrial services. Founded in 1983 and headquartered in Madrid, it operates in more than 50 countries.
English.acs.mission.body: "Develop essential infrastructures for society with excellence, innovation and sustainability, generating value for customers, shareholders and employees."
English.acs.vision.body: "Be the global reference group in infrastructures, driving the ecological and digital transition through sustainable and innovative projects."
English.acs.hist.title: History of ACS
English.acs.hist.p1: ACS is born in Madrid, as a result of the merger of several construction companies, starting its national expansion.
English.acs.hist.p2: Acquisition of Hochtief, German giant of construction, becoming European leader.
English.acs.hist.t3: Company with the highest revenues in the world
English.acs.hist.p3: ACS consolidates its position as world leader in construction, expanding in key markets of Europe and America.
English.acs.hist.p4: Strengthening of the infrastructure concessions area (highways, airports) globally.
English.acs.areas.title: Main Areas
English.acs.area1.title: Construction
English.acs.area1.body: Civil works, building, transport infrastructures (roads, bridges, railways) and singular projects.
English.acs.area2.title: Industrial Services
English.acs.area2.body: Maintenance, installations, industrial engineering, energy and environment for public and private clients.
English.acs.area3.title: Concessions
English.acs.area3.body: Management of highways, airports, hospitals and other infrastructures under concession regime.
English.acs.area4.title: Environment
English.acs.area4.body: Water treatment, waste management, renewable energies and circular economy solutions.
English.acs.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.acs.tech1.title: Sustainability
English.acs.tech1.body: Use of recycled materials, energy efficiency and green certifications.
English.acs.tech2.title: Modular construction
English.acs.tech2.body: Industrialization and prefabrication to reduce deadlines and improve quality.
English.acs.tech3.title: BIM
English.acs.tech3.body: Building Information Modeling in all projects.
English.acs.serv.title: Featured Services
English.acs.prod1.title: Puente de la Constitución
English.acs.prod1.body: One of the longest cable-stayed bridges in Europe, in Cádiz, Spain. Singular engineering project.
English.acs.prod2.title: International concessions
English.acs.prod2.body: Management of highways in Canada, United States and Australia, with quality and sustainability standards.
English.bbva.about.title: About BBVA
English.bbva.what.body: BBVA (Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria) is a global financial group founded in 1857, headquartered in Bilbao and present in more than 30 countries. It offers banking services, insurance and asset management to more than 80 million customers.
English.bbva.mission.body: "Make the opportunities of this new era accessible to everyone, offering innovative and sustainable financial solutions that improve the lives of people and businesses."
English.bbva.vision.body: "Be the bank most valued by our customers, recognized for our excellence, sustainability and leadership in digital transformation."
English.bbva.hist.title: History of BBVA
English.bbva.hist.p1: The Banco de Bilbao is founded, origin of the current BBVA, to finance trade and industry in the Basque Country.
English.bbva.hist.p2: Merger of Banco de Bilbao and Banco de Vizcaya, creating BBV, one of the largest Spanish financial groups.
English.bbva.hist.p3: BBV and Argentaria merge to create BBVA, a global bank with strong presence in Latin America.
English.bbva.hist.p4: BBVA is a leader in banking digital transformation, with a strong commitment to sustainability and financial inclusion.
English.bbva.area1.title: Retail Banking
English.bbva.area1.body: Products and services for individuals, accounts, cards, mortgages and consumer financing.
English.bbva.area2.title: Business Banking
English.bbva.area2.body: Financial solutions for SMEs, large corporations, project financing and treasury.
English.bbva.area3.title: Global Banking
English.bbva.area3.body: Financial markets, investment banking, asset management and private banking internationally.
English.bbva.area4.title: Sustainability
English.bbva.area4.body: Green financing, sustainable bonds, impact investment and climate transition support.
English.bbva.tech1.title: Mobile banking
English.bbva.tech1.body: BBVA App with more than 30 million active users, recognized as the best in the world in 2023.
English.bbva.tech2.title: AI and data
English.bbva.tech2.body: Machine learning models for personalization, fraud prevention and credit scoring.
English.bbva.tech3.title: Open banking
English.bbva.tech3.body: APIs and open platforms to integrate financial services with third parties.
English.bbva.prod1.title: BBVA App
English.bbva.prod1.body: The most valued mobile banking application, with advanced features such as account aggregation, instant payments and voice assistant.
English.bbva.prod2.title: Online mortgage
English.bbva.prod2.body: 100% digital contracting of mortgages, with real-time simulation and electronic signature.
English.dxc.what.body: DXC Technology is a multinational American company of information technology (IT) services, founded in 2017. It specializes in digital transformation and cloud computing.
English.dxc.mission.body: Help organizations modernize their technology, optimize their operations and accelerate their digital transformation through innovative and efficient solutions.
English.dxc.vision.body: Be a global leader in digital and technological services, driving innovation, efficiency and sustainable growth in companies.
English.dxc.hist.p1: DXC was born from the merger between CSC and HPE's enterprise services division, forming one of the largest global IT services companies.
English.dxc.hist.p2: DXC consolidated its strategy in cloud, application modernization, AI and cybersecurity, redefining its business model.
English.dxc.hist.p3: DXC implemented the "Fix, Modernize, Grow" strategy, which allowed stabilizing and modernizing the company, driving its growth.
English.dxc.hist.p4: DXC is a global leader in technology services, with presence in more than 70 countries, standing out in cloud, AI, cybersecurity and automation.
English.unam.about.title: About UNAM
English.unam.what.body:
The National
                  Autonomous University of Mexico (UNAM) is the largest and most important public university in Mexico and one of the most
                  prestigious in Latin America.
English.unam.mission.body:
To train highly
                  qualified professionals, researchers, and academics with social responsibility, capable of contributing
                  to the scientific, technological, cultural, and social development of Mexico.
English.unam.vision.body:
To be a leading university
                  worldwide, recognized for its academic excellence, social impact, scientific innovation, and
                  contribution to sustainable development, maintaining its public character.
English.unam.hist.title: History of UNAM
English.unam.hist.p1:
UNAM is founded on
                  September 22, 1910, on the initiative of Justo Sierra. It marks the birth of the modern university in
                  Mexico and the beginning of organized public higher education in the country.
English.unam.hist.p2:
UNAM obtains its autonomy,
                  allowing it to govern itself, define study plans, and manage its budget. This
                  event consolidates academic freedom and university identity.
English.unam.hist.p3:
University City
                  (CU) is inaugurated, becoming the country's main university campus and an
                  architectural, cultural, and educational symbol of Mexico.
English.unam.hist.p4:
UNAM maintains its position
                  as the best public university in Mexico and one of the most important in Latin America, standing out
                  in research, technological innovation, digital education, and social commitment.
English.unam.areas.title: Main Areas
English.unam.area1.title: Education
English.unam.area1.body:
It is the main function of UNAM: to train high school,
                undergraduate, and graduate students.
English.unam.area2.title: Research
English.unam.area2.body:
UNAM is the main center of scientific and humanistic research in
                Mexico.
English.unam.area3.title: Cultural Dissemination
English.unam.area3.body:
UNAM promotes art, culture, and critical thinking in
                society.
English.unam.area4.title:
Outreach and social
                commitment
English.unam.area4.body: It seeks to apply knowledge for the benefit of society.
English.unam.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.unam.tech1.title: Information Technology (IT)
English.unam.tech1.body:
They support education, research, and
                  university management with their large data center.
English.unam.tech2.title: Cloud Computing
English.unam.tech2.body:
E-learning platforms (Moodle, Google
                  Classroom, Microsoft Teams).
English.unam.tech3.title: AI
English.unam.edu.title: Outstanding Education
English.unam.prod1.title: UNAM Digital Education Platform
English.unam.prod1.body:
The most
                  important educational product of UNAM. Massive open online courses (MOOCs) on platforms like Coursera, edX, and its own
                  UNAM Global platform.
English.unam.prod2.title: Miztli Supercomputer
English.unam.prod2.body:
The most
                  advanced scientific-technological product. One of the most powerful supercomputers in Latin America.
English.cap.what.body: Capgemini is a French multinational company specializing in consulting, technology services, and digital transformation, founded in 1967. It helps organizations modernize their processes, systems, and business models using advanced technologies such as cloud computing, artificial intelligence, big data, and cybersecurity.
English.cap.mission.body: To help businesses transform and grow through the use of technology, offering innovative solutions that improve their efficiency, competitiveness, and sustainability.
English.cap.vision.body: To be a global leader in digital transformation, technological innovation, and business consulting, creating a sustainable future through the responsible use of technology.
English.cap.hist.p1: Capgemini was founded in 1967 in Grenoble, France, by Serge Kampf under the name Sogeti.
English.cap.hist.p2: Capgemini acquired the consulting division of Ernst & Young, representing a huge strategic leap.
English.cap.hist.p3: Capgemini acquired Altran, strengthening its presence in digital engineering.
English.cap.hist.p4: Capgemini is today one of the world's largest companies in consulting and IT services, with a presence in more than 50 countries.
English.cap.area1.title: Digital Transformation
English.cap.area1.body: Digitization of operations, process automation, improvement of customer experience, etc.
English.cap.area2.title: AI
English.cap.area2.body: Solutions based on artificial intelligence and machine learning to optimize processes.
English.cap.area3.title: Cloud Computing
English.cap.area3.body: Support for companies in the migration and management of cloud systems, using platforms like AWS.
English.cap.area4.title: Cybersecurity
English.cap.area4.body: Protection of organizational information and systems through advanced digital security strategies.
English.cap.tech1.body: Capgemini works primarily with this technology to host, scale, and manage systems.
English.cap.tech2.title: Machine Learning
English.cap.tech2.body: Uses intelligent automation to optimize processes and tasks.
English.cap.tech3.title: Apache Hadoop
English.cap.tech3.body: Use of advanced analytics tools to analyze large volumes of data.
English.cap.prod1.title: Capgemini Invent
English.cap.prod1.body: Capgemini's innovation and digital transformation platform to help companies redesign their business model using technology.
English.cap.prod2.title: Capgemini Engineering
English.cap.prod2.body: Its engineering and technological innovation platform, positioning it as a leader in industrial automation.
English.inf.what.body: Infosys is an Indian multinational technology consulting and IT services company, founded in 1981. It offers solutions in digital transformation, artificial intelligence, cloud computing, data analytics, and cybersecurity.
English.inf.mission.body: To help organizations achieve their goals through innovative, efficient, and sustainable digital solutions, driving their technological growth.
English.inf.vision.body: To be a global leader in digital transformation, recognized for innovation, business ethics, and technological excellence.
English.inf.hist.p1: Infosys was founded in India by N. R. Narayana Murthy, aiming to provide high-quality software and technology consulting services.
English.inf.hist.p2: It became one of the first Indian companies to list on the NYSE, achieving international recognition and prestige.
English.inf.hist.p3: The company accelerated its transition towards cloud, big data, artificial intelligence, and automation.
English.inf.hist.p4: Infosys is today one of the largest technology consulting firms in the world, with a presence in over 50 countries and leadership in AI, cloud, and advanced analytics.
English.inf.area1.title: AI
English.inf.area1.body: Infosys develops solutions based on artificial intelligence and machine learning to analyze data and automate processes.
English.inf.area2.title: Cloud Computing
English.inf.area2.body: Infosys supports companies in the migration, management, and optimization of cloud systems using various platforms.
English.inf.area3.title: Digitization
English.inf.area3.body: Infosys helps companies modernize their processes, systems, and business models through digital solutions.
English.inf.area4.title: Cybersecurity
English.inf.area4.body: Infosys offers advanced solutions for protecting data, networks, and systems, including attack prevention.
English.inf.tech1.title: AWS
English.inf.tech1.body: Infosys works primarily with AWS because it allows secure and efficient migration, management, and scaling of enterprise systems.
English.inf.tech2.title: AI
English.inf.tech2.body: Infosys uses AI to analyze data, automate processes, and improve decision-making.
English.inf.tech3.title: Databricks
English.inf.tech3.body: It uses this technology to process, store, and analyze large volumes of enterprise data.
English.inf.prod1.title: Infosys Nia
English.inf.prod1.body: Infosys' artificial intelligence and automation platform, focused on advanced analytics.
English.inf.prod2.title: Infosys Cobalt
English.inf.prod2.body: Infosys' cloud digital transformation platform, focused on cloud migration and management.
English.nv.what.body: NVIDIA is a leader in accelerated computing, artificial intelligence, and graphics solutions, driving innovation in gaming, data centers, and autonomous technologies.
English.nv.mission.body: To develop technologies that amplify human capability and enable solving the world's most complex problems through AI and high-performance computing.
English.nv.vision.body: To be the foundational technology platform that powers the next era of artificial intelligence, the metaverse, and autonomous computing.
English.nv.hist.p1: Jensen Huang founded the company with a vision for the future of computing.
English.nv.hist.p2: Introduction of the GeForce 256, the world's first graphics processing unit.
English.nv.hist.p3: Revolutionizing parallel computing and scientific processing.
English.nv.hist.p4: Leading the generative AI revolution at global scale with next-generation chips.
English.nv.area1.title: Gaming
English.nv.area1.body: GeForce RTX technologies for the ultimate gaming experience.
English.nv.area2.title: Data Center
English.nv.area2.body: AI and HPC solutions for enterprise data centers.
English.nv.area3.title: Automotive
English.nv.area3.body: Autonomous driving and intelligent AI cockpits.
English.nv.area4.title: Healthcare
English.nv.tech1.title: DLSS 3.5
English.nv.tech1.body: AI-powered Deep Learning Super Sampling for unmatched performance.
English.nv.tech2.title: CUDA
English.nv.tech2.body: General-purpose parallel computing architecture to accelerate computation.
English.nv.tech3.title: Ray Tracing
English.nv.tech3.body: Real-time physical light simulation for cinematic realism.
English.nv.prod.title: Products
English.nv.prod1.title: Blackwell Architecture
English.nv.prod1.body: The world's most powerful AI processor, designed for the new era of trillion-scale generative artificial intelligence.
English.nv.prod2.title: GeForce RTX 40 Series
English.nv.prod2.body: Beyond fast. Powered by the Ada Lovelace architecture and DLSS 3 for gamers and creators.
English.sam.what.body: Samsung is a South Korean multinational company founded in 1938, specialized in consumer electronics, information technology, semiconductors, and telecommunications.
English.sam.mission.body: "To inspire the world with innovative technologies, products, and designs that enrich people's lives and contribute to a sustainable future."
English.sam.vision.body: "To become a global leader in technological innovation, responsible and sustainable, positively transforming people's lives and society."
English.sam.hist.p1: Lee Byung-chul founded Samsung in South Korea, starting as a trading company. This year marks the birth of one of the world's largest conglomerates.
English.sam.hist.p2: Samsung Electronics is founded, beginning its technological era and eventually becoming a world leader in consumer electronics and semiconductors.
English.sam.hist.p3: Samsung launches the Galaxy series, positioning itself as a global leader in the smartphone market and marking its dominance in the mobile sector.
English.sam.hist.p4: Samsung consolidates as one of the most influential technology companies in the world, a leader in chips, displays, smartphones, 5G, and artificial intelligence.
English.sam.area1.title: Electronics
English.sam.area1.body: It is Samsung's most visible and commercial area with its smartphones and televisions.
English.sam.area2.title: Semiconductors
English.sam.area2.body: Samsung is a world leader in manufacturing memory chips and processors.
English.sam.area3.title: Visual Technology
English.sam.area3.body: Samsung is a global leader in display technology, from QLED to AMOLED screens.
English.sam.area4.title: Telecommunications
English.sam.area4.body: It is key in the development of next-generation connectivity with its 5G infrastructure and mobile networks.
English.sam.tech1.title: Advanced Semiconductors
English.sam.tech1.body: Samsung is a world leader in semiconductors with its DRAM and NAND memory chips.
English.sam.tech2.title: OLED, AMOLED, and MicroLED Displays
English.sam.tech2.body: Samsung is a pioneer in display technology with its flexibility and foldable capabilities.
English.sam.tech3.title: AI
English.sam.tech3.body: Enhances cameras with facial recognition, performance, and user experience with its virtual assistants.
English.sam.prod1.title: Samsung Galaxy S
English.sam.prod1.body: It is Samsung's flagship product in the mobile market, setting trends in mobile design and technology.
English.sam.prod2.title: Samsung Smart TV QLED
English.sam.prod2.body: It is Samsung's strongest product in home entertainment, offering image quality, AI, and advanced connectivity.
English.gri.about.title: About Grifols
English.gri.what.body: Grifols is a global pharmaceutical company founded in 1909 in Barcelona. It is a leader in the production of plasma-derived medicines and diagnostics, with a presence in more than 30 countries.
English.gri.mission.body: "To improve people's health and well-being through innovation in plasma proteins, diagnostics, and medical technology."
English.gri.vision.body: "To be the world leader in plasma medicine and diagnostics, driving science to save lives."
English.gri.hist.title: History of Grifols
English.gri.hist.p1: José Antonio Grifols founded the first clinical analysis laboratory in Barcelona, the origin of the company.
English.gri.hist.p2: Beginning of serum and plasma derivative production in Spain.
English.gri.hist.p3: Opening of subsidiaries in Europe and the United States, consolidating global presence.
English.gri.hist.p4: Grifols is a global benchmark in plasma medicines, diagnostics, and biotechnological research.
English.gri.areas.title: Main Areas
English.gri.area1.title: Bioscience (Plasma Derivatives)
English.gri.area1.body: Production of albumin, immunoglobulins, coagulation factors, and other plasma derivatives.
English.gri.area2.title: Diagnostics
English.gri.area2.body: Systems and reagents for clinical, transfusion, and food safety analysis.
English.gri.area3.title: Hospital
English.gri.area3.body: Intravenous solutions, parenteral nutrition, and medical devices.
English.gri.area4.title: Biopharma
English.gri.area4.body: Research and development of new biological therapies and innovative medicines.
English.gri.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.gri.tech1.title: Plasma Fractionation
English.gri.tech1.body: Advanced processes to separate and purify plasma proteins on an industrial scale.
English.gri.tech2.title: Molecular Diagnostics
English.gri.tech2.body: PCR and sequencing technologies to detect infectious and genetic diseases.
English.gri.tech3.title: Automation
English.gri.tech3.body: Robotic systems for clinical analysis and sample processing in laboratories.
English.gri.prod.title: Featured Products
English.gri.prod1.title: Albumin
English.gri.prod1.body: Plasma protein used in burns, surgery, and critical care. Grifols is one of the world's leading producers.
English.gri.prod2.title: Immunoglobulins
English.gri.prod2.body: Treatment for immunodeficiencies, autoimmune, and neurological diseases.
English.wmt.about.title: About Walmart
English.wmt.what.body: Walmart is the world's largest retail company, founded in 1962 in the United States. It operates thousands of physical stores and e-commerce platforms in dozens of countries.
English.wmt.mission.body: Save people money so they can live better.
English.wmt.vision.body: To be a global leader in omnichannel retail, integrating physical stores, e-commerce, and advanced technology, while maintaining a commitment to sustainability.
English.wmt.hist.title: History of Walmart
English.wmt.hist.p1: Sam Walton founded Walmart in Arkansas, USA, with the goal of offering low prices every day, creating a business model that transformed global retail.
English.wmt.hist.p2: Walmart begins its expansion outside the United States, starting with Mexico. This year marks the beginning of its global growth.
English.wmt.hist.p3: Walmart becomes for the first time the company with the highest revenue worldwide, consolidating its global leadership in the retail sector.
English.wmt.hist.p4: Walmart consolidates as a leader in omnichannel retail, integrating physical stores, e-commerce, logistics automation, artificial intelligence, and data analytics.
English.wmt.area1.title: Physical Retail
English.wmt.area1.body: It is the core of Walmart's business.
English.wmt.area2.title: E-commerce
English.wmt.area2.body: Key to competing with Amazon and strengthening the omnichannel model. They use their website and app.
English.wmt.area3.title: Logistics
English.wmt.area3.body: Allows maintaining low prices and high product availability through its distribution centers.
English.wmt.area4.title: Financial Services
English.wmt.area4.body: Area focused on facilitating payments and affordable services.
English.wmt.tech1.title: Automation
English.wmt.tech1.body: Increases efficiency, reduces costs and delivery times with robots in its distribution centers.
English.wmt.tech2.title: Cloud Computing
English.wmt.tech2.body: Supports its e-commerce, logistics, and global operations on hybrid cloud infrastructure.
English.wmt.tech3.title: AI
English.wmt.tech3.body: Enables intelligent real-time decisions with machine learning that predicts demand.
English.wmt.prod1.title: Walmart Pickup & Delivery
English.wmt.prod1.body: The most important and used service. It is key to competing with Amazon and leading the omnichannel model.
English.wmt.prod2.title: Walmart Financial Services
English.wmt.prod2.body: Walmart's most important financial service. Very important in markets such as Mexico and Latin America, where it promotes financial inclusion.
English.tsl.what.body: Tesla is an American multinational technology and automotive company, founded in 2003, specializing in the design, manufacture, and sale of electric vehicles, batteries, and clean energy solutions.
English.tsl.mission.body: To accelerate the world's transition to sustainable energy.
English.tsl.vision.body: To create a future powered by clean, sustainable, and accessible energy for everyone.
English.tsl.hist.p1: Tesla was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning in California. This year marks the birth of the company, with the goal of proving that electric cars could be fast.
English.tsl.hist.p2: In 2008, Tesla launched the Roadster, its first commercial electric car. This event was crucial because it demonstrated that electric vehicles could be powerful and modern.
English.tsl.hist.p3: In 2012, Tesla introduced the Model S, a luxury electric sedan that was a huge global success. This year consolidated Tesla as a leader in the electric car industry and revolutionized the market with advanced technology.
English.tsl.hist.p4: Tesla remains a global leader in electric cars, with significant advances in autonomous driving and mass production.
English.tsl.area1.title: Automotive
English.tsl.area1.body: It is Tesla's main area. It includes the design, manufacture, and sale of electric vehicles.
English.tsl.area2.title: Energy
English.tsl.area2.body: Focuses on clean energy solutions such as solar panels and solar roof tiles.
English.tsl.area3.title: Autonomous Driving
English.tsl.area3.body: Focuses on the production of Autopilot and AI chip development.
English.tsl.area4.title: Robotics
English.tsl.area4.body: Development of the humanoid robot called Optimus.
English.tsl.tech1.title: High-Density Batteries
English.tsl.tech1.body: Lithium-ion batteries and 4680 cells enable greater range, efficiency, and lifespan.
English.tsl.tech2.title: AI
English.tsl.tech2.body: Deep neural networks and computer vision used in Autopilot.
English.tsl.tech3.title: Embedded Systems
English.tsl.tech3.body: Proprietary operating systems and centralized architecture that function as a computer on wheels.
English.tsl.prod1.title: Tesla Model 3
English.tsl.prod1.body: It is the vehicle that massively popularized Tesla.
English.tsl.prod2.title: Cybertruck
English.tsl.prod2.body: It is Tesla's most innovative and disruptive vehicle.
English.gm.about.title: About General Motors
English.gm.what.body: General Motors (GM) is a leading American multinational automotive company, founded in 1908. It designs, manufactures, and markets cars, trucks, SUVs, and electric vehicles.
English.gm.mission.body: "To deliver mobility experiences that are safer, smarter, and more sustainable for everyone."
English.gm.vision.body: "A world with zero crashes, zero emissions, and zero congestion."
English.gm.hist.title: History of General Motors
English.gm.hist.p1: William C. Durant founded General Motors, beginning one of the world's most important automotive companies.
English.gm.hist.p2: GM becomes the largest automobile manufacturer on the planet, surpassing Ford and dominating the industry for decades.
English.gm.hist.p3: GM enters controlled bankruptcy and is restructured, marking a key turning point for its survival and modernization.
English.gm.hist.p4: General Motors consolidates as a leader in electric vehicles, automotive software, and sustainable innovation, with strong investment in AI, electrification, and autonomous driving.
English.gm.areas.title: Main Areas
English.gm.area1.title: Vehicle Design and Manufacturing
English.gm.area1.body: It is the core of GM's business.
English.gm.area2.title: Electric Mobility
English.gm.area2.body: Key area for GM's sustainable future with its development of electric vehicles.
English.gm.area3.title: Autonomous Driving
English.gm.area3.body: Focused on the future of intelligent mobility with advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS).
English.gm.area4.title: Financial Services
English.gm.area4.body: Facilitates access to its products and services with its GM Financial tool.
English.gm.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.gm.tech1.title: Electric Vehicles
English.gm.tech1.body: Foundation of GM's electric future. They use the Ultium platform.
English.gm.tech2.title: Autonomous Driving
English.gm.tech2.body: Enables semi-autonomous and safe driving. They use Super Cruise.
English.gm.tech3.title: AI
English.gm.prod.title: Featured Products
English.gm.prod1.title: Chevrolet Silverado
English.gm.prod1.body: It is GM's most emblematic vehicle in the pickup segment. It is one of the best-selling trucks in North America.
English.gm.prod2.title: Chevrolet Bolt EV
English.gm.prod2.body: It is GM's most representative electric model. It was one of the first accessible and mass-market electric cars.
English.rep.about.title: About Repsol
English.rep.what.body: Repsol is a global energy company founded in 1987, headquartered in Madrid. It operates across the entire value chain: exploration and production, refining, chemicals, marketing, renewables, and new energy vectors.
English.rep.mission.body: "To be a global energy company committed to sustainability, innovation, and value creation, offering efficient and low-emission energy products and services."
English.rep.vision.body: "To be a net zero emissions company by 2050, a leader in the energy transition and a benchmark in sustainable value creation."
English.rep.hist.title: History of Repsol
English.rep.hist.p1: Repsol is created as a result of the restructuring of the Spanish public oil sector, grouping exploration, refining, and marketing assets.
English.rep.hist.p2: Repsol acquires YPF, consolidating itself as one of the largest energy companies in Latin America and expanding its global presence.
English.rep.hist.p3: Beginning of the energy transition strategy with investments in renewable energies, mainly wind and solar.
English.rep.hist.p4: Repsol advances towards the goal of net zero emissions by 2050, with a diversified portfolio in hydrocarbons, renewables, green hydrogen, and biofuels.
English.rep.areas.title: Main Areas
English.rep.area1.title: Exploration and Production (E&P)
English.rep.area1.body: Upstream activities: exploration, development, and production of hydrocarbons worldwide.
English.rep.area2.title: Industrial (Refining and Chemicals)
English.rep.area2.body: Operation of refineries, petrochemical plants, and production of fuels, asphalt, and chemical products.
English.rep.area3.title: Commercial & Renewables
English.rep.area3.body: Network of service stations, sale of electricity and gas, and development of solar, wind, and hydrogen projects.
English.rep.area4.title: Innovation & Technology
English.rep.area4.body: Research centers for advanced biofuels, circular economy, CO₂ capture, and new materials.
English.rep.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.rep.tech1.title: Renewable Energies
English.rep.tech1.body: Wind farms, solar plants, and large-scale green hydrogen projects.
English.rep.tech2.title: Biofuels
English.rep.tech2.body: Production of fuels from organic waste and vegetable oils.
English.rep.tech3.title: Digitalization
English.rep.tech3.body: Digital twins, AI, and big data to optimize refineries and asset management.
English.rep.prod.title: Featured Products
English.rep.prod1.title: Service Stations
English.rep.prod1.body: More than 4,800 stations in Spain and Portugal, with high-quality fuels and mobility services.
English.rep.prod2.title: Advanced Biofuels
English.rep.prod2.body: Renewable fuels produced from waste, compatible with current engines.
English.bim.about.title: About Bimbo
English.bim.what.body: Grupo Bimbo is a leading Mexican multinational company in the baking industry, founded in 1945 in Mexico City. It specializes in the production, distribution, and marketing of bread.
English.bim.mission.body: To nourish a better world by offering high-quality, nutritious, accessible, and reliable products for everyone.
English.bim.vision.body: To be a highly productive and fully humane company, a world leader in the food industry, committed to sustainability and innovation.
English.bim.hist.title: History of Bimbo
English.bim.hist.p1: Year in which Grupo Bimbo was founded in Mexico City, marking the beginning of a company that would become the world's largest bakery.
English.bim.hist.p2: Bimbo began its expansion outside Mexico, entering international markets such as the United States and Latin America, marking the start of its global growth.
English.bim.hist.p3: Grupo Bimbo consolidated as the largest bakery company in the world, with presence on multiple continents and a vast production and distribution network.
English.bim.hist.p4: Bimbo is today a leading multinational in the food industry, with presence in more than 30 countries, focused on innovation, sustainability, and nutrition, under its purpose of "Nourishing a better world".
English.bim.areas.title: Main Areas
English.bim.area1.title: Production
English.bim.area1.body: Area responsible for the production of bread, pastries, snacks, and baked goods, using advanced industrial processes and high standards of quality, hygiene, and food safety.
English.bim.area2.title: Logistics
English.bim.area2.body: Bimbo has one of the largest distribution networks in the world, allowing it to deliver its products quickly, efficiently, and safely to stores.
English.bim.area3.title: Innovation
English.bim.area3.body: This area focuses on the creation of new products, nutritional improvements, development of healthier recipes, and adaptation to consumer needs, betting on food innovation.
English.bim.area4.title: Sustainability
English.bim.area4.body: Bimbo promotes environmental care programs, use of renewable energy, emission reduction, recycling, and community support.
English.bim.tech.title: Key Technologies
English.bim.tech1.title: Automation
English.bim.tech1.body: Bimbo uses automated production lines, industrial robots, and control systems to manufacture bread and other products with high efficiency.
English.bim.tech2.title: AI
English.bim.tech2.body: They use AI to predict demand and optimize inventories with the help of big data to make better decisions.
English.bim.tech3.title: Internet of Things
English.bim.tech3.body: It uses smart sensors to monitor machinery, temperature, humidity, and production processes, ensuring quality.
English.bim.plat.title: Featured Platforms
English.bim.prod1.title: Bimbo White Bread
English.bim.prod1.body: It is the flagship product of the Bimbo brand and one of the most consumed sliced breads in Mexico and Latin America.
English.bim.prod2.title: Gansito Marinela
English.bim.prod2.body: It is one of the most iconic and popular snack cakes in Mexico.
English.coke.about.title: About Coca-Cola
English.coke.what.body: Coca-Cola is a leading American multinational company in the beverage industry, founded in 1886. It is recognized for its flagship drink, Coca-Cola.
English.coke.mission.body: "To refresh the world in mind, body, and spirit. To inspire moments of optimism and happiness through our brands and actions. To create value and make a difference."
English.coke.vision.body: "To be a total beverage company, driving sustainable growth, caring for the planet, and improving people's quality of life."
English.coke.hist.title: History of Coca-Cola
English.coke.hist.p1: John Stith Pemberton creates Coca-Cola in Atlanta. This year marks the birth of the world's most famous drink.
English.coke.hist.p2: Asa Candler officially founds The Coca-Cola Company, establishing the foundations for business growth and global expansion.
English.coke.hist.p3: The contour bottle is introduced, becoming a universal symbol of the brand and a key element of its identity.
English.coke.hist.p4: Coca-Cola consolidates as a world leader in beverages, focused on innovation, sustainability, and healthier products.
English.coke.area1.title: Carbonated Beverages
English.coke.area1.body: It is the company's main and most traditional business.
English.coke.area2.title: Non-Carbonated Beverages
English.coke.area2.body: Key area due to the trend towards healthier drinks.
English.coke.area3.title: Bottling and Distribution
English.coke.area3.body: Allows Coca-Cola to reach more than 200 countries with its production and packaging.
English.coke.area4.title: Marketing
English.coke.area4.body: Keeps Coca-Cola as a leading and modern brand with its global advertising and environmental care.
English.coke.tech1.title: Robotics
English.coke.tech1.body: Enables fast, safe, and efficient production with its industrial robots and quality control systems.
English.coke.tech2.title: Big Data
English.coke.tech2.body: Improves decision-making and commercial strategy with its great inventory optimization.
English.coke.tech3.title: AI
English.coke.tech3.body: Increases operational efficiency with marketing personalization and sales predictions.
English.coke.prod1.title: Coca Cola
English.coke.prod1.body: It is the flagship product and symbol of the brand. It is the world's most famous drink.
English.coke.prod2.title: Fanta
English.coke.prod2.body: It is Coca-Cola's second most important product. It is one of the best-selling soft drinks worldwide.

Je recommande de positionner notre offre de cloud privé comme une alternative souveraine différenciée à la dépendance aux hyperscaleurs.
